database_metadata.name: hepatitis
database_metadata.description: TODThis data is a modified version of the PKDD02 Discovery Challenge database which includes removing tests with null values. It contains data on the laboratory examinations of hepatitis B and C infected patients. The examinations were realized between 1982 and 2001 on 771 patients. The data are organized in 7 tables (4 entity tables, 3 relationship tables and 16 descriptive attributes). They contain basic information about the patients, results of biopsy, information on interferon therapy, results of out-hospital examinations, results of in-hospital examinations.

tables.description: Biopsy-related table containing histological findings for hepatitis cases, including fibrosis stage and inflammatory activity.

columns.name: fibros
columns.comment: Liver fibrosis stage stored as a discretized categorical value. It has 5 ordered levels with range 0-4, corresponding to fibrosis stages F0-F4.

columns.name: activity
columns.comment: Histological inflammatory activity grade stored as a discretized categorical value. In this standardized version it is represented as 5 ordered levels with range 0-4;

columns.name: b_id
columns.comment: Primary key of the Bio table, representing the biopsy record ID.

qa_hints.table_summary: Table `bio` has 32 rows. Biopsy-related table containing histological findings for hepatitis cases, including fibrosis stage and inflammatory activity.
column_summaries: fibros (BIGINT): Liver fibrosis stage stored as a discretized categorical value. It has 5 ordered levels with range 0-4, corresponding to fibrosis stages F0-F4. Range [0.0, 4.0], avg=2.2188, median=2.0.

activity (BIGINT): Histological inflammatory activity grade stored as a discretized categorical value. In this standardized version it is represented as 5 ordered levels with range 0-4;  Range [0.0, 4.0], avg=2.1562, median=2.0.
b_id (BIGINT): Primary key of the Bio table, representing the biopsy record ID.

tables.description: TODOCentral patient/case table containing the main entity for hepatitis classification, including patient identifier, demographic attributes, and the target label.

columns.name: m_id
columns.comment: Primary key of the dispat table and target ID of the dataset.

columns.name: sex
columns.comment: Sex of the patient, stored as a categorical varchar value.Usually: 1 = Male, 2 = Female

columns.name: age
columns.comment: Age of the patient, stored as a discretized categorical varchar value rather than a continuous number.Mapping relationships: 0: <20 years old, 1: 20-29 years old, 2: 30-39 years old, 3: 40-49 years old, 4: 50-59 years old, 5: 60-69 years old, 6: 70+ years old

columns.name: type
columns.comment: Target label of the dataset, representing the hepatitis type classification.0 = Hepatitis B (B), 1 = Hepatitis C (C)

qa_hints.table_summary: Table `dispat` has 500 rows. TODOCentral patient/case table containing the main entity for hepatitis classification, including patient identifier, demographic attributes, and the target label.
column_summaries: m_id (BIGINT): Primary key of the dispat table and target ID of the dataset.
sex (BIGINT): Sex of the patient, stored as a categorical varchar value.Usually: 1 = Male, 2 = Female Range [0.0, 1.0], avg=0.29, median=0.0.

age (BIGINT): Age of the patient, stored as a discretized categorical varchar value rather than a continuous number.Mapping relationships: 0: <20 years old, 1: 20-29 years old, 2: 30-39 years old, 3: 40-49 years old, 4: 50-59 years old, 5: 60-69 years old, 6: 70+ years old Range [0.0, 6.0], avg=4.4, median=5.0.
type (BIGINT): Target label of the dataset, representing the hepatitis type classification.0 = Hepatitis B (B), 1 = Hepatitis C (C) Range [0.0, 1.0], avg=0.588, median=1.0.

tables.description: In-hospital examination table containing discretized laboratory test results for hepatitis cases.

columns.name: got
columns.comment: Serum GOT/AST (aspartate aminotransferase), stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 5 levels with range 0-4, where larger values indicate higher enzyme levels.

columns.name: gpt
columns.comment: Serum GPT/ALT (alanine aminotransferase), stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate higher enzyme levels.

columns.name: alb
columns.comment: Albumin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher albumin level.

columns.name: tbil
columns.comment: Total bilirubin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher total bilirubin level.

columns.name: dbil
columns.comment: Direct bilirubin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher direct bilirubin level.

columns.name: che
columns.comment: Cholinesterase, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 10 levels with range 0-9, where larger values indicate a higher cholinesterase level.

columns.name: ttt
columns.comment: Thymol turbidity test, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 6 levels with range 0-5, where larger values indicate a higher test result.

columns.name: ztt
columns.comment: Zinc sulfate turbidity test, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 6 levels with range 0-5, where larger values indicate a higher test result.

columns.name: tcho
columns.comment: Total cholesterol, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate a higher cholesterol level.

columns.name: tp
columns.comment: Total protein, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate a higher total protein level.

columns.name: in_id
columns.comment: Primary key of the indis table, representing the in-hospital examination record ID.

qa_hints.table_summary: Table `indis` has 5691 rows. In-hospital examination table containing discretized laboratory test results for hepatitis cases.
column_summaries: got (BIGINT): Serum GOT/AST (aspartate aminotransferase), stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 5 levels with range 0-4, where larger values indicate higher enzyme levels. Range [0.0, 4.0], avg=1.8608, median=2.0.

gpt (BIGINT): Serum GPT/ALT (alanine aminotransferase), stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate higher enzyme levels. Range [0.0, 3.0], avg=1.4521, median=1.0.

alb (BIGINT): Albumin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher albumin level. Range [0.0, 1.0], avg=0.3024, median=0.0.

tbil (BIGINT): Total bilirubin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher total bilirubin level. Range [0.0, 1.0], avg=0.8512, median=1.0.

dbil (BIGINT): Direct bilirubin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher direct bilirubin level. Range [0.0, 1.0], avg=0.4157, median=0.0.

che (BIGINT): Cholinesterase, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 10 levels with range 0-9, where larger values indicate a higher cholinesterase level. Range [0.0, 9.0], avg=5.8419, median=6.0.

ttt (BIGINT): Thymol turbidity test, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 6 levels with range 0-5, where larger values indicate a higher test result. Range [0.0, 5.0], avg=3.5758, median=4.0.

ztt (BIGINT): Zinc sulfate turbidity test, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 6 levels with range 0-5, where larger values indicate a higher test result. Range [0.0, 5.0], avg=3.1186, median=3.0.

tcho (BIGINT): Total cholesterol, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate a higher cholesterol level. Range [0.0, 3.0], avg=1.3217, median=1.0.

tp (BIGINT): Total protein, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate a higher total protein level. Range [0.0, 3.0], avg=1.4767, median=1.0.
in_id (BIGINT): Primary key of the indis table, representing the in-hospital examination record ID.

tables.description: Interferon-therapy table containing treatment-related information for hepatitis cases.

columns.name: dur
columns.comment: Duration of interferon therapy, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 5 levels with range 0-4; larger values indicate a longer treatment duration.

columns.name: a_id
columns.comment: Primary key of the inf table, representing the interferon-treatment record ID.

qa_hints.table_summary: Table `inf` has 196 rows. Interferon-therapy table containing treatment-related information for hepatitis cases.
column_summaries: dur (BIGINT): Duration of interferon therapy, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 5 levels with range 0-4; larger values indicate a longer treatment duration. Range [0.0, 4.0], avg=2.0102, median=2.0.
a_id (BIGINT): Primary key of the inf table, representing the interferon-treatment record ID.

tables.description: Relation table linking hepatitis cases in `dispat` to biopsy records in `bio`.

columns.name: b_id
columns.comment: Foreign key referencing `bio.b_id`, identifying the biopsy record linked to a hepatitis case.

columns.name: m_id
columns.comment: Foreign key referencing `dispat.m_id`, identifying the hepatitis case linked to a biopsy record.

m_id (BIGINT): Foreign key referencing `dispat.m_id`, identifying the hepatitis case linked to a biopsy record.

m_id (BIGINT): Foreign key referencing `dispat.m_id`, identifying the hepatitis case linked to an examination record.
